public_goals: Avoid being sidelined in child-activity decisions.
Keep the process fair and workable rather than rigid.
Reduce repeated conflict over how decisions are communicated.
private_concerns: Feels some decisions are framed as already settled before they can respond.
Wants a process that protects fairness without inviting endless delay.
Does not want respect concerns to be dismissed as emotional overreaction.
red_lines: Will not accept a communication process that turns into one-sided control.
Will not accept activity decisions being treated as final before meaningful notice is given.
emotional_triggers: being informed after decisions already feel final
process tools framed as one-sided control
disclosure_tendencies: shares practical process ideas once mutuality is explicit
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes sharper if the process sounds like after-the-fact compliance
becomes more constructive when fairness and notice are framed as shared obligations
starting_positions: Primary position: child-activity decisions should not leave one parent feeling sidelined or informed after the fact.
Secondary position: written summaries are acceptable if they support fairness rather than control.
likely_openings: I do not want to keep finding out about things after they are basically decided.
If we write things down, it needs to work for both of us.
